commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=524517c15f59c17ebcc2c8cb731b8e6215e26616)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` contract and passes runtime settings into context-engine lifecycle calls across harness, CLI, embedded, and Codex app-server paths.

PR surface: Source +267, Tests +111. Total +378 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current `main` strips only `sessionKey` and `prompt` for strict legacy context engines, while this PR passes a new `runtimeSettings` property into lifecycle params. A strict engine that rejects unknown keys follows the existing tested strict-engine pattern and would enter the runtime quarantine fallback path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This PR needs contributor-visible code fixes and real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not repair it automatically while the proof gate is still missing.

target.title: feat(context-engine): pass runtime settings into lifecycle
target.body:
## Summary

Adds an OpenClaw-owned `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` contract and passes it into context-engine lifecycle calls.

This is intentionally framed as a general OpenClaw context-engine programming contract, not a HyperMem-specific shim. Core now builds a typed runtime settings object from the active host/runtime/model context and supplies it to lifecycle hooks so context engines do not need to infer runtime facts from globals, logs, provider strings, or side channels.

What changed:

- Adds exported `ContextEngineRuntimeSettings` plus typed unavailable/unsupported runtime-settings errors.
- Adds `buildContextEngineRuntimeSettings()` in `src/context-engine/runtime-settings.ts`.
- Passes `runtimeSettings` into `bootstrap`, `assemble`, `afterTurn`, and `maintain` params.
- Wires runtime settings through the shared harness lifecycle, Codex app-server attempt path, embedded runner attempt path, generic CLI runner path, and deferred context-engine maintenance.
- Keeps lifecycle param additions optional for legacy context-engine compatibility.

## Validation

Initial validation before the `origin/main` refresh was run from a clean worktree branch based on `openclaw/openclaw:main` at `9d54285b0d`:

```text
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/context-engine/runtime-settings.test.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts
pnpm check:test-types
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts src/agents/cli-runner.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/context-engine-maintenance.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.ts src/context-engine/runtime-settings.test.ts src/context-engine/runtime-settings.ts src/context-engine/types.ts
git diff --check
pnpm lint --threads=8
```

Observed result on `9d54285b0d`:

- Focused Vitest: 2 shards passed, 13 tests passed.
- `pnpm check:test-types`: passed.
- `oxfmt --check`: passed on touched files.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`: passed.

After rebasing cleanly onto current `origin/main` at `6653193fdb`:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/context-engine/runtime-settings.test.ts src/agents/harness/context-engine-lifecycle.test.ts
git diff --check
pnpm check:test-types
```

Observed result after rebase:

- Focused Vitest: 2 shards passed, 13 tests passed.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- `pnpm check:test-types`: failed in existing current-main Tailscale test typing, outside this PR diff:
  - `src/gateway/server-tailscale.test.ts(108,48): Argument of type '"node.tailnet.ts.net"' is not assignable to parameter of type 'null'.`
  - `src/gateway/server-tailscale.test.ts(130,48): Argument of type '"node.tailnet.ts.net"' is not assignable to parameter of type 'null'.`
  - `src/gateway/server-tailscale.test.ts(154,48): Argument of type '"100.64.0.8"' is not assignable to parameter of type 'null'.`
  - `src/gateway/server-tailscale.test.ts(169,48): Argument of type '"node"' is not assignable to parameter of type 'null'.`

## Notes

This is the narrow lifecycle spine. Follow-up work can extend the public SDK docs/exports and richer doctor display after the active slot/compaction PRs settle, but the core runtime-settings contract is useful independently.
